title: Be Glad
content:
Be glad, just for to-day!
    O heart, be glad!
    Cast all your cares away!
    Doff all that 's sad!
    Put of your garments gray
    Be glad to-day!
    Be merry while you-can;
    For life is short
    It seemeth but a span
    Before we part.
    Let each maid take her man,
    And dance while dance she can:
    Life's but a little span
    Be merry while you can.
meaning:
Madison Julius Cawein's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Be Glad"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
